behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real filesystem watch behavior (recursive watch coverage), which depends on OS + watcher library semantics. A mocked watcher or direct function call can hide missed subdirectory events. Rejected lower level: behavior text explicitly requires detection of files placed in real subdirectories under the watched root.
behaviors.test_description: Start the daemon against a real temp directory tree, create a nested subdirectory, write a file there, and assert exactly one transformed output is produced for that file path.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily module handoff/state behavior (ingest request -> dedup window state -> enqueue/drop) and can fail even without OS events. Real OS/network/subprocess is not required, and no AI output dependency exists. Rejected System: behavior text is about dedup logic and configurable window, not OS-specific event mechanics.
behaviors.test_description: Wire ingest + dedup + queue modules in-process with a controllable clock, submit the same normalized path twice within the configured window, and assert first request enqueues and second is dropped; then assert acceptance after window expiry.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly names OS-emitted duplicate events; this failure appears at real watcher/OS boundary. Mocked event streams may miss timing/coalescing quirks that cause duplicate processing in production. Rejected Integration: concrete text requires real OS emission pattern.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon with a real watched directory, perform a write pattern known to emit multiple events (e.g., temp-write+rename or multi-chunk write), and assert only one pipeline execution/output for that file.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: Requires real filesystem lifecycle events (delete/recreate/inode change) while long-running watcher is active. In-process fakes can hide re-subscription and OS watcher invalidation failures. Rejected Integration: behavior text explicitly depends on runtime directory removal/recreation.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon watching a real temp directory, delete the directory, recreate it at same path, then add a new file and assert it is processed without restarting the daemon.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Ordering correctness depends on interaction between real file arrivals, timestamp metadata retrieval/resolution, and scheduler handoff. Mock metadata/order can mask production failures from FS timestamp granularity and event races. Rejected Integration: behavior text ties order to file creation timestamps under concurrent arrival conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Create multiple files nearly simultaneously in a real watched directory with controlled creation times, capture processing order, and assert daemon processes according to creation timestamp ordering rule.

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: This is a real filesystem timing/visibility boundary (partial writes vs readiness). Mock writes usually present fully materialized content and can hide premature pickup bugs. Rejected Integration: behavior explicitly depends on in-progress OS-level file writes at event time.
behaviors.test_description: Begin a slow multi-chunk write to a file in the watched directory, verify daemon does not process during partial write, then complete/close write and assert single processing occurs afterward with full content.
